_README: Ordered backlog of EXISTING landmark studies to teach, one per slot. The routine takes queue[0], writes the lesson, and moves that entry into done[]. Topics are interleaved so the cadence rotates. Add more any time; keep them real, well-documented, and teachable to a curious non-scientist. After the queue runs dry (or after a couple months), flip to surfacing NEW studies — see routines/.

queue.study: Silver et al. (2016), 'Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search', Nature
queue.angle: How AlphaGo beat a game thought to be a decade away from machines.

queue.study: Kox et al. (2014), 'Voluntary activation of the sympathetic nervous system and attenuation of the innate immune response in humans', PNAS
queue.angle: The Wim Hof study: breathing and cold trained people to consciously dial down inflammation.

queue.study: Cepeda et al. (2006), 'Distributed practice in verbal recall tasks', Psychological Bulletin
queue.angle: The spacing effect: the same study time spread out beats it crammed together, every time.

queue.study: Matsuda et al. (1990), 'Structure of a cannabinoid receptor and functional expression of the cloned cDNA', Nature
queue.angle: Finding the lock that THC fits — the receptor that proved the body was built to respond to cannabis.

queue.study: Kahneman, Knetsch & Thaler (1990), 'Experimental Tests of the Endowment Effect and the Coase Theorem', Journal of Political Economy
queue.angle: The coffee-mug experiment: why owning something instantly makes you value it more.

queue.study: Karikó & Weissman (2005), 'Suppression of RNA Recognition by Toll-like Receptors', Immunity
queue.angle: The quiet 2005 fix that made mRNA vaccines possible — and won a Nobel 18 years later.

queue.study: Krizhevsky, Sutskever & Hinton (2012), 'ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks' (AlexNet), NeurIPS
queue.angle: The 2012 result that kicked off the entire deep-learning era.

queue.study: Chang et al. (2015), 'Evening use of light-emitting eReaders negatively affects sleep', PNAS
queue.angle: Hard evidence that screens before bed push your body clock later and blunt melatonin.

queue.study: Ericsson, Krampe & Tesch-Römer (1993), 'The Role of Deliberate Practice in the Acquisition of Expert Performance', Psychological Review
queue.angle: The real study behind the (often-misquoted) '10,000 hours' idea.

queue.study: Fuss et al. (2015), 'A runner's high depends on cannabinoid receptors in mice', PNAS
queue.angle: The 'runner's high' may owe more to your endocannabinoids than to endorphins.

queue.study: Shiller (1981), 'Do Stock Prices Move Too Much to be Justified by Subsequent Changes in Dividends?', American Economic Review
queue.angle: The evidence that markets are far more volatile than rational pricing can explain.

queue.study: Arute et al. (2019), 'Quantum supremacy using a programmable superconducting processor', Nature
queue.angle: Google's claim that a quantum chip did in minutes what a supercomputer would take millennia to do.

queue.study: Mnih et al. (2015), 'Human-level control through deep reinforcement learning', Nature
queue.angle: One algorithm that learned to play dozens of Atari games from raw pixels and a score.

queue.study: Colman et al. (2009), 'Caloric Restriction Delays Disease Onset and Mortality in Rhesus Monkeys', Science
queue.angle: The decades-long monkey study at the heart of the eat-less-live-longer debate.

queue.study: Watts, Duncan & Quan (2018), 'Revisiting the Marshmallow Test', Psychological Science
queue.angle: The famous willpower study gets re-run with 10x the kids — and the story changes.

queue.study: Gaoni & Mechoulam (1964), 'Isolation, Structure, and Partial Synthesis of an Active Constituent of Hashish', JACS
queue.angle: The moment a chemist finally pinned down what THC actually is.

queue.study: Tversky & Kahneman (1974), 'Judgment under Uncertainty: Heuristics and Biases', Science
queue.angle: The mental shortcuts that quietly run your decisions — anchoring, availability, representativeness.

queue.study: Watson & Crick (1953), 'Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids', Nature
queue.angle: One page in Nature that revealed the double helix and launched modern biology.

queue.study: Goodfellow et al. (2014), 'Generative Adversarial Networks', NeurIPS
queue.angle: Two neural networks playing a forgery game — the idea behind a decade of AI image generation.

queue.study: Davidson et al. (2003), 'Alterations in Brain and Immune Function Produced by Mindfulness Meditation', Psychosomatic Medicine
queue.angle: An 8-week meditation course left measurable marks on the brain and the immune response.

queue.study: Slamecka & Graf (1978), 'The Generation Effect', Journal of Experimental Psychology
queue.angle: You remember words you generate yourself far better than words you simply read.

queue.study: Volkow et al. (2014), 'Adverse Health Effects of Marijuana Use', NEJM
queue.angle: A sober, evidence-based accounting of cannabis risks — especially for the developing brain.

queue.study: Fama (1970), 'Efficient Capital Markets: A Review of Theory and Empirical Work', Journal of Finance
queue.angle: The efficient-market hypothesis: the claim that you can't beat the market on public info.

queue.study: Mayor & Queloz (1995), 'A Jupiter-mass companion to a solar-type star', Nature
queue.angle: The first planet found orbiting another sun-like star — and the method that found thousands more.

done: 2026-06-17-am — frontier — Abbott et al. / LIGO (2016), Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Merger (Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102)
2026-06-15-am — cannabis — Devinsky et al. (2017), Trial of Cannabidiol for Drug-Resistant Seizures in the Dravet Syndrome (NEJM)
2026-06-14-pm — learning — Kruger & Dunning (1999), Unskilled and Unaware of It
2026-06-14-am — biohacking — Xie et al. (2013), Sleep Drives Metabolite Clearance (glymphatic system)
2026-06-13-pm — ai — Jumper et al. (2021), AlphaFold protein structure prediction
2026-06-12-pm — ai — Vaswani et al. (2017), Attention Is All You Need
2026-06-12-am — biohacking — Hatori et al. (2012), time-restricted feeding
2026-06-15-pm — finance — Markowitz, H. (1952), "Portfolio Selection," The Journal of Finance 7(1), 77-91
